description: render the 5 fixed benchmark PNG charts from a benchmark_data.json — trtexec BS=1 vs BS=MAX, GPU throughput, DS streams-vs-fps, trt-vs-ds, and pipeline efficiency
rules: Exactly 5 charts with fixed filenames (chart_trtexec_bs1_vs_bsmax.png, chart_trtexec_throughput.png, chart_ds_streams_vs_fps.png, chart_trt_vs_ds.png, chart_efficiency.png) — the report HTML/PDF references these exact names; do not rename
trtexec imgs_per_sec must be > 0 — efficiency cannot be computed against a zero baseline and the chart step fails fast
Charts are rendered headless (matplotlib Agg backend) at 150 dpi
usage: Set BENCHMARK_DATA to the benchmark_data.json path (model_name, trtexec bs1/bsmax, peak_gpu_streams, deepstream run1/run2) and optionally CHART_DIR (defaults to ${RECORD_STORE}/charts). Output: the 5 chart_*.png files plus benchmark-charts.json (audit manifest with the chart count).
limitation: Requires the `matplotlib` python package; without it the perk degrades gracefully (no PNGs, charts_written=0). The JSON must contain all required keys — partial data raises a KeyError that is logged and the run degrades.
